title: How do you evaluate retrieval in a way that matches user experience?
prompt:
How do you evaluate retrieval in a way that matches user experience?

*Answer in the editor as you would out loud in an interview (a few sentences to a short paragraph is fine). Then use 💡 Hint, 🧠 AI feedback, or 📗 Show answer.*
reference_solution:
Build a golden dataset of 30-50 real queries with annotated ground-truth documents and ideal answers. Layer three evaluation methods: retrieval metrics (Precision@k, MRR, NDCG), end-to-end metrics via LLM-as-Judge (faithfulness, answer relevance, completeness), and periodic human evaluation on production samples.

**Takeaway:** Golden dataset + automated LLM-as-Judge + periodic human review. No single metric is enough.
hint_notes: Golden dataset + automated LLM-as-Judge + periodic human review. No single metric is enough.
